skill.name: obsidian-theme-designer
skill.description: Use when the user wants to design, preview, or customize an Obsidian vault theme — including choosing styles, comparing color schemes, adjusting typography, or generating CSS snippets. Triggers on keywords like "Obsidian theme", "color scheme", "CSS snippet", "appearance".
skill.summary: Design Obsidian themes visually with browser previews, then generate importable CSS snippets with automatic font installation.
skill.author: XiangyuSu611

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 124 potential issues, but evaluation confirms the vast majority are false positives. The skill is a legitimate Obsidian theme design tool. Backtick 'execution' matches are markdown inline code formatting for CSS variables. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' matches are false positives triggered by 'MD' substrings in CSS/Markdown context. The 'Windows SAM database' match is a false positive where 'Sample' was flagged. Network and filesystem access are justified: the skill downloads Google Fonts to ~/.local/share/fonts (Linux), ~/Library/Fonts (macOS), or Windows Fonts directory, which is the documented and expected behavior. PowerShell invocation is legitimate Windows font registration code.

low_findings.title: PowerShell font registration on Windows
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:177 documents using PowerShell to register fonts in the Windows registry (HKCU). This is standard, documented behavior for installing fonts on Windows, executed in the user's own user context (HKCU, not HKLM). Not malicious.

content.value_statement: Designing an Obsidian theme usually requires CSS knowledge and trial-and-error. This skill provides a visual, guided workflow with browser previews so any user can create a custom theme with the right colors, fonts, and layout.
actual_capabilities: Extracts theme CSS variables from existing Obsidian themes using grep
Launches a visual companion browser preview via the superpowers plugin
Guides users through style direction selection with visual mockups
Generates bilingual (Chinese/English) preview content for font showcase
Downloads and installs Google Fonts to the system (Windows/macOS/Linux)
Produces a ready-to-import CSS snippet file in .obsidian/snippets/
limitations: Requires the superpowers plugin for browser-based visual previews
Font installation requires system-level access (writes to OS font directories)
Only generates CSS snippets, not full custom themes (relies on a base theme)
Visual companion requires a local server process to be running

use_cases.title: Theme customization for Obsidian users
use_cases.description: Users who want a personalized Obsidian appearance but lack CSS skills can use this skill to visually design and implement a custom theme.

use_cases.title: Bilingual content theming
use_cases.description: Researchers and writers working with mixed Chinese and English content can preview and configure font pairings that render both scripts well.

use_cases.title: Rapid theme prototyping
use_cases.description: Designers iterating on Obsidian themes can use the visual preview workflow to test multiple style directions and color palettes quickly.

prompt_templates.title: Basic theme design
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to start the full theme design workflow

prompt_templates.title: Font-only customization
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to change the fonts in my Obsidian theme to something distinctive. Help me pick new fonts.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to update only the fonts

prompt_templates.title: Dark mode focused
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a dark mode theme for my Obsidian vault. I prefer cool tones and minimal decoration.

prompt_templates.title: Reference-based design
prompt_templates.prompt: I like the look of this website [attach screenshot]. Help me create a similar Obsidian theme.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a visual reference to emulate

output_examples.input: Design my Obsidian theme. I prefer warm tones and academic style.
output_examples.output: Step 1: Choose style direction — Academic selected (like a LaTeX PDF). Step 2: Color palette — warm earth tones applied. Step 3: Fonts — Source Serif 4 for body, Noto Serif SC for CJK. Step 4: Preview shows light/dark side by side. Step 5: CSS snippet generated to .obsidian/snippets/warm-academic.css

output_examples.input: I want a dark-only theme for coding, with cool tones.
output_examples.output: Step 1: Style direction — Dark immersive selected. Step 2: Cool palette (teal accents). Step 3: JetBrains Mono for code, DM Sans for UI. Step 4: Dark preview confirmed. Step 5: Fonts downloaded and installed. CSS snippet saved and ready to enable.

best_practices: Always show visual previews in the browser rather than asking users to pick from text descriptions
Ask for reference images first before showing style options to avoid unnecessary questions
Include bilingual content in previews to verify font rendering for both scripts
Unify font families between light and dark modes — only change colors, not typography
anti_patterns: Asking users to pick hex colors or font names directly in the terminal
Using generic fonts like Arial, Inter, or Roboto without considering distinctive alternatives
Showing style options as text labels without visual mockups
Generating CSS without a visual preview confirmation step

faq.question: What is this skill used for?
faq.answer: It guides you through designing a custom Obsidian theme visually, then generates a CSS snippet you can import into Obsidian.

faq.question: Do I need CSS knowledge?
faq.answer: No. The skill handles all CSS generation. You make visual choices and the skill writes the code.

faq.question: What plugins are required?
faq.answer: The superpowers plugin is required for browser-based visual previews. The frontend-design plugin is recommended for font selection.

faq.question: Does it install fonts automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. After you approve a font selection, it downloads the font files from Google Fonts and installs them to your system font directory.

faq.question: Where is the CSS snippet saved?
faq.answer: It is saved to the .obsidian/snippets/ directory inside your vault. You enable it from Obsidian Settings > Appearance > CSS Snippets.

faq.question: Can I use it with bilingual Chinese and English content?
faq.answer: Yes. All previews include mixed Chinese and English text to verify that font pairings work for both scripts.
